---
title: "suspected kernal crash"
date: 2010-03-08
forum: General Help
---

### Post by CyberJacob on 2010-03-08
I belive that my kernal keeps crashing, it appears to be random, the activity light on the case is perminantly on, everything freezes, and i meen EVERYTHING, after a few minits the caps lock and scr lk (?) lights start to flash.
the only way to solve it is to unplug the computer and plug it back in again.
i can provide logs (you'll have to direct me to them) but I'm not sure how much use they'll be.
please help as this is really annoying and i keep looseing all of my work.

---

### Post by Frogs Hair on 2010-03-08
Hi,
 
Click system, drop down to administration, drop down to logs and select kernel log 
move log to date of events. Any errors should be recorded there.

---

### Post by CyberJacob on 2010-03-08
the only way I can boot up for more than a minite or two is to use my live cd, so I've done that, i have no idea what I'm looking for in the log file (i couldnt find kernal.log in /var/logs so i'm using syslog instead) so I'll just copy and paste it.

### Post by AgentZ86 on 2010-03-10
I suspect the same on my 9.10 karmic machine:

I look forward to a method to diagnose this whether it's a software topic or hardware failure. I may not have kernel crash at all, but perhaps hard drive failing, however Gparted checks the disk as OK.

Anyhow, I look forward to any help on this subject as well, and would love to learn about this.

Thanks

---

### Post by CyberJacob on 2010-03-10
I'm not sure if they're related or not, mine deffinately boots. sometimes I'm on a terminal when it happens and I get loads of error messages, the last one (only one I can remember) is:
```

[time code thingy] kernal panic, sync error: tryed to kill init

```
maby it's tryin to be a rap-star?;):rolleyes:

### Post by Maverick7687 on 2010-03-13
I am having the same problem. I can't do anything to reproduce it other than just using the machine as I normally do and it will eventually lock up. Sometimes it restarts on its own and sometimes I have to cut the power. I was suspecting a HDD problem but it seems this is more widespread than I thought. I have included my Kernel log and for this period of time the lockup has happened twice. Once in the 16:00 hour and once around 17:30.

### Post by CyberJacob on 2010-03-14
I thaught it was hdd related, but some research on the internet shows that it may be memory related. btw did you have a windows instalation that 'died' on this computer before you installed ubuntu? if so this problem is verry wide-spread over a LOT of forums!!! just search on the internet for the kernal panic message.

---

### Post by CyberJacob on 2010-03-25
thaught I had fixed it :p but then id did it again :(

---

### Post by Gary_inNYC on 2010-09-09
I have this issue on a fresh install of 10.04 on an IBM Thinkpad t40.  It has happened approximately 4 times.  The computer would randomly crash to blackscreen, and nothing would respond.  It's a recurring issue indicated by a blinking caps lock light on the laptop.  I'd show you some logs, but where to begin?

Looking back in the forums, it appears the blinking caps lock blackscreen thing has been around since 2008.  

Anyways, there was a recent kernel upgrade I installed through update manager (I installed the image meta package and its corresponding headers), and it appears that the random crashing has stopped, or at least it hasn't happened yet since the upgrade.  This is all anecdotal, since the random occurrence of this crash means it's a wait-and-see type of thing.  So far it's been about a week since I've last seen the crash.

If it happens again, I'll make sure to document date, time, sift through logs and throw them into a pastebin.  

- Gary_inNYC

---
